evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows 4 methods × 5 benchmarks with error bars; winner (OursModel★) is visually highlighted via bold edge, bold legend label, and star; readable at 89mm single-column width.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' correctly set; font sizes reasonable (8pt base, 7.5pt tick, 6.5pt legend); however DejaVu Sans default is used instead of a preferred journal font (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or Times).
evidence.palette: Wong 2011 colorblind-safe palette (#999999, #56B4E9, #E69F00, #D55E00) used; no red/green directional misuse; hatching added for B&W readers.
evidence.layout_density: y_min computed dynamically (not forced to 0), tight_layout pad=0.4, aspect ratio 0.72, 2-column legend placed upper-left; no wasted whitespace or label overlaps detected.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure and explains error bars and winner claim; legend inside plot with bold winner; missing: no numerical accuracy value in caption to anchor the claim; hatch on 4th method (OursModel) is empty string same as baseline, reducing B&W discriminability.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed real vector: decompressed stream contains PDF path operators (m, l, h, f); TrueType fonts (DejaVu Sans) embedded via /FontFile2; no raster image xobject present.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (44 KB), figure.png (70 KB), figure.svg (172 KB), caption.tex, gen_figure.py, brief.md.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies all 5 benchmarks, states error bar meaning, and asserts OursModel wins on every benchmark; however, no specific numerical accuracy value is cited (e.g., 'achieving X% on MMLU'), which weakens the quantitative claim.

overall_strengths: Correct colorblind-safe palette (Wong 2011) with hatching for B&W accessibility, and winner clearly distinguished via bold edge weight and legend formatting.
Real vector PDF with fonttype=42 TrueType embedding, dynamic y-range, and compact single-column layout meeting Nature/CVPR formatting requirements.
overall_weaknesses: DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default font and is not appropriate for publication; a journal-quality font (e.g., Helvetica/Arial or a serif like Times New Roman) should be specified.

Caption lacks a concrete numerical anchor (no specific accuracy percentage cited), and the 4th method (OursModel) shares the same empty hatch pattern as the baseline method, undermining B&W discriminability.
